Even if you have PGDE, it does not guarantee a teaching position
If you can get into PCLL, most likely you will be able to secure a training contract
I think the salary of a NQ at a small local firm should be 35k up, unless you/the firm is shit.
PQE 5, around 55k up la. The unless proviso applies.

Money wise, eventually you will earn more as a lawyer than as a teacher, even after taking into a/c of the tuition fees.
The only thing you have to consider is, do you want to be a lawyer? Do you prefer to be a lawyer to a teacher? You should have believed so when you started studying JD. You can still change your mind before paying PCLL tuition fees. Once you have done that, it is not easy to change course.
